Branding · Visual Identity · 2022

fullscreen

Client
Fullscreen
Role
Brand Identity
Year
2022

Bold.
Primary.
Uncompro-mising.

Fullscreen is a brand built on directness. The logotype — set in a heavy sans-serif with a distinctive corner bracket mark — communicates confidence, precision, and frame-filling presence. No decoration, no apology.

The primary colour palette draws from the Bauhaus tradition: black, cobalt blue, crimson red, and cadmium yellow. Each colour is autonomous and equal — the mark shifts between them depending on context, always legible, always commanding.

Brand Identity Logo Design Colour System Typography Manual da Marca

Logotype

01

The logotype pairs a bold sans-serif wordmark with a geometric corner bracket — an isotype that works independently at any scale. The bracket references a screen frame, a crop mark, a threshold.

Colour

02

Four autonomous primaries — each sufficient on its own, each equal to the others. The palette is rooted in Bauhaus principles: pure hue, maximum contrast, zero compromise.

Helvetica Neue for display and headlines — direct, industrial, without personality that competes with content. Azo Sans for body copy — clean, efficient, invisible.

Typeface
Helvetica
Neue
Aa
Bold

Fullscreen is a brand built on direct, industrial communication — no decoration, no noise. Every surface is engineered for clarity, speed, and maximum legibility across every screen size and context.

Fullscreen is a brand built on direct, industrial communication — no decoration, no noise. Every surface is engineered for clarity, speed, and maximum legibility across every screen size and context.

Applications

04

Founded in 2000 — when the web was still raw and uncharted — Fullscreen carries that origin in its DNA. The identity leans into it deliberately: archival footage, scan-line textures, the grain of early digital culture. Not nostalgia for its own sake, but a statement that longevity is rare in this industry, and that experience is the differentiator. The mark applies across surfaces with the same confidence it had on day one.
